title: Red Sox continue to get bad news on Roman Anthony’s recovery from lingering wrist injury
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox outfielder Roman Anthony remains sidelined with a wrist injury since May 4, delaying his return.
Anthony's recovery from a sprained wrist/hand has been slow, causing frustration for both the player and team.
Initial expectations for Anthony's injury were a short absence, but torn ligaments now require up to 12 weeks to heal.

title: Celtics select St. John’s wing Dillon Mitchell with 40th pick in 2026 NBA Draft
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Celtics pick St. John's wing Dillon Mitchell with the 40th selection in the 2026 NBA Draft.
Mitchell, known for defense, played for Texas, Cincinnati, and St. John's, earning All-Big East honors.
Former Celtics player Isaiah Thomas announced Mitchell's selection during the draft proceedings.

title: Boy, 13, hospitalized after being found unresponsive in swimming pool at Beverly home
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 13-year-old boy was found unresponsive in a swimming pool at a Beverly home on Wednesday.
Bystanders and first responders administered CPR before the boy was flown to a Boston hospital.
Beverly police have launched an investigation into the incident at the Parramatta Road residence.

title: Here’s where every tall ship will be docked for Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 tall ships will dock across Charlestown, downtown Boston, and the Seaport for Sail Boston 250 from July 11-16.
Notable ships include Italy's Amerigo Vespucci, Argentina's Libertad, and Germany's Gorch Fock, located mainly in the Seaport District.
Public ship viewing is available daily from July 12-15, with variable boarding opportunities based on each ship's readiness.

title: Windhorst: ‘Very good chance’ Jaylen Brown isn’t on Celtics next season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Jaylen Brown remains at the center of trade talks as Celtics explore reshaping their roster.
ESPN insider Brian Windhorst suggests a strong possibility Brown won't be with the Celtics next season.
Boston offered Brown and two first-round picks for Giannis Antetokounmpo, who was traded to the Miami Heat.

title: WCVB weeknight meteorologist Mike Wankum will retire end of June, station announces
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mike Wankum, WCVB's weeknight meteorologist for over 20 years, will retire at the end of June.
Chief Meteorologist Cindy Fitzgibbon will succeed Wankum in the weeknight spot starting July. Kelly Ann Cicalese shifts to weekdays.
Wankum, the longest-serving broadcast meteorologist in Boston, has earned 17 regional Boston/New England Emmy Awards.

title: 14 years later, man charged in death of 7-year-old in Central Mass.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Steven Stuart, 35, charged with murder of Jayden Carlson, who died in 2017 from injuries sustained in 2012.
Stuart was previously convicted in 2015 for assaulting Jayden, served six to eight years in prison.
Jayden Carlson endured medical complications for five years after the assault, lived in a nursing home, and died at age 7.

title: Is your favorite Boston bar packed with World Cup revelers? Here are some FIFA-free alternatives.
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Grendel's Den in Cambridge offers a World Cup-free atmosphere without televisions.
Long Bar & Terrace at Raffles Boston provides a TV-free rooftop bar experience.
Blossom Bar at Sichuan Garden in Brookline remains quiet during World Cup broadcasts.

title: Man allegedly stole nearly $2,000 from Medford church donation boxes, police say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Michael Bagley, 60, was arrested for allegedly stealing nearly $2,000 from Medford church donations, police reported.
Bagley faces charges including larceny over $1,200 and breaking into a depository; he pleaded not guilty in court.
Police suspect Bagley stole from churches in Medford and surrounding cities over a month, collecting evidence since May.

title: Celtics guard Derrick White is taking a basketball job with his alma mater at Colorado
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Derrick White named 'President of Basketball Strategy' at University of Colorado, aiding in athlete development and team culture.
White donates $2 million to University of Colorado's men's basketball program, supporting revenue opportunities and donor relations.
White, key Celtics guard, previously averaged 18.1 points in his season with Colorado, joining from University of Colorado-Colorado Springs.
